titleStmt.title: A letter from Francis E. Meehan to Augustine Birrell, 3 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Francis Meehan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a copy of a letter from Francis E. Meehan (?-1946), MP for North Leitrim in 1916, to the Chief Secretary of Ireland Augustine Birrell (1850-1933). In the letter Francis E. Meehan is asking Birrell to consider Mr. Thomas E. Delany (1868-1939), crown solicitor for Longford, for the newly vacant post of Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland. The post had been held by Sir Malachy Kelly (1851-1916) before his sudden death.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6258/5

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
MANORHAMILTON, CO.LEITRIM. 3rd April 1916. Dear Mr. Birrell, I beg
to recommend
Mr. J.W. Delany
Crown Solicitor
Longford for the
position of Chief
Crown Solicitor
for Ireland and
trust you may
favourably consider
his application
for the position. I am
Yours very faithfully F. E. Meehan
(M.P. North Leitrim)
Rt. Hon. A. Birrell M.P.
Chief Secretary
